title: useReactNativePlatformComponents
description: Learn more about useReactNativePlatformComponents

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="JavaScript (and super languages)" icon="seti:javascript">
:::caution
This rule is part of the [nursery](/linter/#nursery) group. This means that it is experimental and the behavior can change at any time.
:::
## Summary
- Rule available since: `v2.4.13`
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/nursery/useReactNativePlatformComponents`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**error**](/reference/diagnostics#error).
- This rule belongs to the following domains:
  - [`reactNative`](/linter/domains#reactNative)
- Sources: 
  - Inspired from [`react-native/split-platform-components`](https://github.com/Intellicode/eslint-plugin-react-native/blob/master/docs/rules/split-platform-components.md)

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useReactNativePlatformComponents": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Ensure that platform-specific React Native components are only
imported in files named for that platform.

Some React Native components only work on one platform. For example,
`ProgressBarAndroid` is Android-only and `ActivityIndicatorIOS` is
iOS-only. These components should live in files with a matching
platform suffix such as `.android.js` or `.ios.js`, so the React
Native bundler can ship the right code to each platform.

This rule reports an error when a platform-specific component is
imported in a file that does not have the matching suffix, or when
both Android and iOS components are imported in the same file.

## Examples

### Invalid

Importing an Android component in a non-Android file:

```js
import { ProgressBarAndroid } from "react-native";
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.js:1:10 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-react-native-platform-components">lint/nursery/useReactNativePlatformComponents</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">✖</span></strong> <span style="color: Tomato;">Android component </span><span style="color: Tomato;"><strong>ProgressBarAndroid</strong></span><span style="color: Tomato;"> is used outside of an Android-specific file.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>import &#123; ProgressBarAndroid &#125; from &quot;react-native&quot;;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>         <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Platform-specific components produce incorrect bundles when imported in shared files.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Move this import to a file with an Android-specific suffix (e.g. </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>.android.js</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;">).</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

Importing an iOS component in a non-iOS file:

```js
import { ActivityIndicatorIOS } from "react-native";
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.js:1:10 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-react-native-platform-components">lint/nursery/useReactNativePlatformComponents</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">✖</span></strong> <span style="color: Tomato;">iOS component </span><span style="color: Tomato;"><strong>ActivityIndicatorIOS</strong></span><span style="color: Tomato;"> is used outside of an iOS-specific file.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>import &#123; ActivityIndicatorIOS &#125; from &quot;react-native&quot;;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>         <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Platform-specific components produce incorrect bundles when imported in shared files.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Move this import to a file with an iOS-specific suffix (e.g. </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>.ios.js</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;">).</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

### Valid

```js
import { View } from "react-native";
```

## Options

### `androidPathPatterns`

A list of glob patterns to identify Android-specific files.

Default: `["**/*.android.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}"]`

In the following example, Android files use `.droid.jsx` as their suffix instead of the default `.android.js`:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useReactNativePlatformComponents": {
					"options": {
						"androidPathPatterns": [
							"**/*.droid.jsx"
						]
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```jsx title='Button.droid.jsx'
import { ProgressBarAndroid } from "react-native";
```

```jsx title='Button.android.jsx'
import { ProgressBarAndroid } from "react-native";
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///Button.android.jsx">/Button.android.jsx</a>:1:10 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-react-native-platform-components">lint/nursery/useReactNativePlatformComponents</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">✖</span></strong> <span style="color: Tomato;">Android component </span><span style="color: Tomato;"><strong>ProgressBarAndroid</strong></span><span style="color: Tomato;"> is used outside of an Android-specific file.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>import &#123; ProgressBarAndroid &#125; from &quot;react-native&quot;;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>         <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Platform-specific components produce incorrect bundles when imported in shared files.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Move this import to a file with an Android-specific suffix (e.g. </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><strong>.android.js</strong></span><span style="color: lightgreen;">).</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

### `iosPathPatterns`

A list of glob patterns to identify iOS-specific files.

Default: `["**/*.ios.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}"]`

In the following example, iOS files use `.apple.jsx` as their suffix instead of the default `.ios.js`:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"useReactNativePlatformComponents": {
					"options": {
						"iosPathPatterns": [
							"**/*.apple.jsx"
						]
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```jsx title='Button.apple.jsx'
import { ActivityIndicatorIOS } from "react-native";
```

```jsx title='Button.ios.jsx'
import { ActivityIndicatorIOS } from "react-native";
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///Button.ios.jsx">/Button.ios.jsx</a>:1:10 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/use-react-native-platform-components">lint/nursery/useReactNativePlatformComponents</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">✖</span></strong> <span style="color: Tomato;">iOS component </span><span style="color: Tomato;"><strong>ActivityIndicatorIOS</strong></span><span style="color: Tomato;"> is used outside of an iOS-specific file.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>import &#123;
